name: aviation_safety_defense_subcontractor_harbor_st_lt
premise: A Aviation Safety response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving aviation safety defense subcontractor harbor st lt. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as short term vs long term under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Marco Chandra
agents.identity: I am Marco Chandra, accountable for a defensible call in Aviation Safety when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays long term for short term.

agents.name: Mei Diallo
agents.identity: I am Mei Diallo, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Aviation Safety.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so long term isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of short term.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect long term.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Marco Chandra must decide under short term vs long term. What does Marco Chandra do?
